name: French B2 Grammar
description: Master CEFR B2 French grammar — scoped from the Référentiel B2 pour le français (Beacco et al., Didier). Fill in the blank to prove you know which form fits.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le professeur est surpris que je n'{___} si longtemps.
(The teacher is surprised that I spoke so long.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The teacher is surprised that I spoke so long.
facts.grammarPointLabel: parler (je) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aie parlé — subjonctif passé of parler with avoir. Formation: que j'aie (subj. présent of avoir) + parlé (past participle). Expresses a completed action viewed from the present.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis soulagée que tu {___} tes devoirs à temps.
(I'm relieved that you finished your homework on time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm relieved that you finished your homework on time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: finir (tu) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aies fini — subjonctif passé of finir. Formation: que tu aies (subj. présent of avoir) + fini (past participle). The completed action precedes the current feeling of relief.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle est étonnée qu'il {___} une telle décision sans la consulter.
(She is astonished that he made such a decision without consulting her.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She is astonished that he made such a decision without consulting her.
facts.grammarPointLabel: prendre (il) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ait pris — subjonctif passé of prendre (irregular past participle: pris). Formation: qu'il ait + pris. The decision was made before the present astonishment.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il regrette que nous lui {___} la vérité trop tard.
(He regrets that we told him the truth too late.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He regrets that we told him the truth too late.
facts.grammarPointLabel: dire (nous) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ayons dit — subjonctif passé of dire (irregular past participle: dit). Formation: que nous ayons + dit. After regretter que, the past subjunctive marks the action as completed.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous sommes heureux que vous {___} le voyage sans encombre.
(We are happy that you made the trip without any problems.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We are happy that you made the trip without any problems.
facts.grammarPointLabel: faire (vous) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ayez fait — subjonctif passé of faire (irregular past participle: fait). Formation: que vous ayez + fait. The completed trip precedes the current happiness.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis déçu qu'ils n'{___} aucun message d'excuse.
(I am disappointed that they wrote no message of apology.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am disappointed that they wrote no message of apology.
facts.grammarPointLabel: écrire (ils) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aient écrit — subjonctif passé of écrire (irregular past participle: écrit). Formation: qu'ils aient + écrit. The absence of writing is a completed past state.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est dommage que nous n'{___} pas le lever du soleil ce matin.
(It's a shame that we didn't see the sunrise this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a shame that we didn't see the sunrise this morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: voir (nous) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ayons vu — subjonctif passé of voir (irregular past participle: vu). Formation: que nous ayons + vu. After c'est dommage que, the past subjunctive marks the missed event as completed.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis contente que tu {___} à la fête hier soir.
(I'm glad that you were able to come to the party last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm glad that you were able to come to the party last night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: pouvoir+venir (tu) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aies pu venir — subjonctif passé of pouvoir + infinitive venir. Formation: que tu aies + pu + venir. Modal verbs use avoir in compound tenses; completed ability is expressed with the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ses parents sont fiers qu'elle {___} son examen du baccalauréat.
(Her parents are proud that she passed her baccalaureate exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Her parents are proud that she passed her baccalaureate exam.
facts.grammarPointLabel: réussir (elle) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ait réussi — subjonctif passé of réussir. Formation: qu'elle ait + réussi. After être fiers que, the completed exam result calls for the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis soulagé que tu {___} enfin le problème.
(I am relieved that you finally understood the problem.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am relieved that you finally understood the problem.
facts.grammarPointLabel: comprendre (tu) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aies compris — subjonctif passé of comprendre (irregular past participle: compris). Formation: que tu aies + compris. The understanding happened before the present relief.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis étonné qu'il {___} tout le roman en une seule nuit.
(I'm amazed that he read the entire novel in one night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm amazed that he read the entire novel in one night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: lire (il) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ait lu — subjonctif passé of lire (irregular past participle: lu). Formation: qu'il ait + lu. The completed reading precedes the present amazement.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sa famille est soulagée qu'elle {___} les résultats de son bilan médical.
(Her family is relieved that she received her medical results.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Her family is relieved that she received her medical results.
facts.grammarPointLabel: recevoir (elle) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ait reçu — subjonctif passé of recevoir (irregular past participle: reçu). Formation: qu'elle ait + reçu. The completed receipt of results precedes the present relief.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis déçu qu'ils n'{___} pas assez de temps dans ce projet.
(I'm disappointed that they didn't put enough time into this project.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm disappointed that they didn't put enough time into this project.
facts.grammarPointLabel: mettre (ils) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aient mis — subjonctif passé of mettre (irregular past participle: mis). Formation: qu'ils aient + mis. After être déçu que, the lack of effort is treated as a completed past state.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est dommage que nous n'{___} pas trouver de compromis.
(It's a shame that we didn't manage to find a compromise.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a shame that we didn't manage to find a compromise.
facts.grammarPointLabel: vouloir (nous) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: ayons voulu — subjonctif passé of vouloir (irregular past participle: voulu). Formation: que nous ayons + voulu. After c'est dommage que, the completed unwillingness calls for the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis désolé que tu {___} ton portefeuille pendant le voyage.
(I'm sorry that you lost your wallet during the trip.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm sorry that you lost your wallet during the trip.
facts.grammarPointLabel: perdre (tu) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aies perdu — subjonctif passé of perdre (regular past participle: perdu). Formation: que tu aies + perdu. After être désolé que, the completed loss requires the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est remarquable qu'ils {___} autant de succès en si peu de temps.
(It's remarkable that they experienced such great success in so little time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's remarkable that they experienced such great success in so little time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: connaître (ils) — Subjonctif passé avoir
facts.explanation: aient connu — subjonctif passé of connaître (irregular past participle: connu). Formation: qu'ils aient + connu. The completed period of success is marked with the past subjunctive after c'est remarquable que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ma mère est contente que je {___} la voir ce week-end.
(My mother is glad that I went to see her this weekend.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My mother is glad that I went to see her this weekend.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aller (je) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: sois allé(e) — subjonctif passé of aller with être. Formation: que je sois (subj. présent of être) + allé(e) (past participle agrees with subject gender). Movement verbs use être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis triste que tu {___} si tôt hier soir.
(I'm sad that you left so early last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm sad that you left so early last night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: partir (tu) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: sois parti(e) — subjonctif passé of partir with être. Formation: que tu sois + parti(e). Past participle agrees with the subject's gender. Used after être triste que to mark a completed departure.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis content qu'elle {___} me rendre visite la semaine dernière.
(I'm glad that she came to visit me last week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm glad that she came to visit me last week.
facts.grammarPointLabel: venir (elle) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soit venue — subjonctif passé of venir with être. Formation: qu'elle soit + venue (past participle agrees: elle → venue). Classic être-verb in compound tenses.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nos parents sont soulagés que nous {___} avant minuit.
(Our parents are relieved that we got back before midnight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Our parents are relieved that we got back before midnight.
facts.grammarPointLabel: rentrer (nous) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soyons rentrés — subjonctif passé of rentrer with être. Formation: que nous soyons + rentrés (agreement with nous for a mixed or masculine group). The return happened before the current relief.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le directeur est étonné qu'ils {___} en retard à la réunion.
(The director is astonished that they arrived late for the meeting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The director is astonished that they arrived late for the meeting.
facts.grammarPointLabel: arriver (ils) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soient arrivés — subjonctif passé of arriver with être. Formation: qu'ils soient + arrivés (masculine plural agreement). After être étonné que, the completed late arrival uses the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est bizarre qu'elle {___} chez elle toute la journée sans appeler.
(It's strange that she stayed home all day without calling.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's strange that she stayed home all day without calling.
facts.grammarPointLabel: rester (elle) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soit restée — subjonctif passé of rester with être. Formation: qu'elle soit + restée (feminine agreement). After c'est bizarre que, the completed action requires the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je doute qu'ils {___} avant la fin du spectacle.
(I doubt that they left before the show ended.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I doubt that they left before the show ended.
facts.grammarPointLabel: sortir (ils) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soient sortis — subjonctif passé of sortir with être. Formation: qu'ils soient + sortis (masculine plural). After douter que, the past subjunctive marks the completed doubted departure.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle est surprise que vous {___} sans lui dire au revoir.
(She is surprised that you left without saying goodbye to her.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She is surprised that you left without saying goodbye to her.
facts.grammarPointLabel: partir (vous) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soyez partis — subjonctif passé of partir with être. Formation: que vous soyez + partis (plural agreement). After être surprise que, the departure already happened, requiring the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est merveilleux qu'elle {___} en bonne santé.
(It's wonderful that she was born healthy.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's wonderful that she was born healthy.
facts.grammarPointLabel: naître (elle) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soit née — subjonctif passé of naître with être. Formation: qu'elle soit + née (feminine agreement). Naître always uses être in compound tenses.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis désolé qu'il {___} et se soit blessé pendant l'entraînement.
(I'm sorry that he fell and hurt himself during training.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm sorry that he fell and hurt himself during training.
facts.grammarPointLabel: tomber (il) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soit tombé — subjonctif passé of tomber with être. Formation: qu'il soit + tombé (masculine singular). After être désolé que, the completed fall requires the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis surpris qu'elle {___} du train à la mauvaise station.
(I'm surprised that she got off the train at the wrong station.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm surprised that she got off the train at the wrong station.
facts.grammarPointLabel: descendre (elle) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soit descendue — subjonctif passé of descendre (intransitive use with être). Formation: qu'elle soit + descendue (feminine agreement). Descendre uses être when intransitive (no direct object).

facts.quizQuestion:
Sa femme est surprise qu'il {___} jusqu'au dernier étage à pied.
(His wife is surprised that he walked up to the top floor.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: His wife is surprised that he walked up to the top floor.
facts.grammarPointLabel: monter (il) — Subjonctif passé être
facts.explanation: soit monté — subjonctif passé of monter (intransitive use with être). Formation: qu'il soit + monté (masculine singular). When monter has no direct object it uses être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mon médecin est content que je {___} tôt ce matin pour l'examen.
(My doctor is glad that I got up early this morning for the examination.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My doctor is glad that I got up early this morning for the examination.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se lever (je) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: me sois levé(e) — subjonctif passé of se lever. Formation: que je me sois + levé(e). Pronominal verbs use être; the reflexive pronoun me stays with the subject. Past participle agrees with subject gender.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sa mère s'étonne qu'elle {___} aussi vite ce matin.
(Her mother is amazed that she got dressed so quickly this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Her mother is amazed that she got dressed so quickly this morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: s'habiller (elle) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: se soit habillée — subjonctif passé of s'habiller. Formation: qu'elle se soit + habillée (feminine agreement). The completed dressing action is viewed from the present amazement.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nos voisins sont choqués que nous {___} si tard hier soir.
(Our neighbours are shocked that we went to bed so late last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Our neighbours are shocked that we went to bed so late last night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se coucher (nous) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: nous soyons couchés — subjonctif passé of se coucher. Formation: que nous nous soyons + couchés (masculine/mixed plural). After être choqués que, the completed bedtime requires the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis surpris qu'il {___} dans ses calculs.
(I am surprised that he made a mistake in his calculations.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am surprised that he made a mistake in his calculations.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se tromper (il) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: se soit trompé — subjonctif passé of se tromper. Formation: qu'il se soit + trompé (masculine singular). The error is a completed event prior to the present surprise.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis ravi qu'elle {___} de notre anniversaire de mariage.
(I'm delighted that she remembered our wedding anniversary.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm delighted that she remembered our wedding anniversary.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se souvenir (elle) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: se soit souvenue — subjonctif passé of se souvenir. Formation: qu'elle se soit + souvenue (feminine: souvenue). Se souvenir always uses être in compound tenses and the past participle agrees with subject gender.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est une chance qu'ils {___} après tant d'années.
(It's fortunate that they found each other again after so many years.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's fortunate that they found each other again after so many years.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se retrouver (ils) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: se soient retrouvés — subjonctif passé of se retrouver. Formation: qu'ils se soient + retrouvés (masculine plural). After c'est une chance que, the completed reunion calls for the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis content que tu {___} suffisamment avant l'opération.
(I'm glad that you rested enough before the operation.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm glad that you rested enough before the operation.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se reposer (tu) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: te sois reposé(e) — subjonctif passé of se reposer. Formation: que tu te sois + reposé(e). The rest is a completed action before the surgery; emotion trigger être content que demands the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est surprenant que nous {___} si vite au décalage horaire.
(It's surprising that we got used to the jet lag so quickly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's surprising that we got used to the jet lag so quickly.
facts.grammarPointLabel: s'habituer (nous) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: nous soyons habitués — subjonctif passé of s'habituer. Formation: que nous nous soyons + habitués. After il est surprenant que, the completed adaptation uses the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nos hôtes sont ravis que nous {___} lors de la soirée.
(Our hosts are delighted that we had a great time at the party.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Our hosts are delighted that we had a great time at the party.
facts.grammarPointLabel: s'amuser (nous) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: nous soyons bien amusés — subjonctif passé of s'amuser. Formation: que nous nous soyons + amusés. Pronominal verb with être; past participle agrees with nous (masculine/mixed plural). The fun is a completed event.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est inquiet qu'elle {___} sans prendre son médicament.
(He is worried that she fell asleep without taking her medication.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He is worried that she fell asleep without taking her medication.
facts.grammarPointLabel: s'endormir (elle) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: se soit endormie — subjonctif passé of s'endormir. Formation: qu'elle se soit + endormie (feminine). After être inquiet que, the completed falling asleep requires the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le professeur est heureux qu'ils {___} au cours de phonétique.
(The teacher is happy that they enrolled in the phonetics course.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The teacher is happy that they enrolled in the phonetics course.
facts.grammarPointLabel: s'inscrire (ils) — Subjonctif passé pronominal
facts.explanation: se soient inscrits — subjonctif passé of s'inscrire (irregular past participle: inscrit). Formation: qu'ils se soient + inscrits (masculine plural). After être heureux que, the completed enrollment requires the past subjunctive.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je doute qu'il {___}.
(I doubt he will come.)
Context: He hasn't come yet — the coming is still in the future.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I doubt he will come.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif présent (futur/en cours)
facts.explanation: "vienne" (subjonctif présent) is correct because the doubted action is still pending/future. Subjonctif présent follows a present-tense main clause when the subordinate action is simultaneous or future.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je doute qu'il {___}.
(I doubt he has come / came.)
Context: The event (his arrival) is already in the past — you're doubting whether it happened.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I doubt that he came.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé (accompli)
facts.explanation: "soit venu" (subjonctif passé) is required because the doubted action is already completed. Subjonctif passé = que + auxiliaire au subjonctif présent + participe passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important que … (choose the correct concordance form)
Context: You want him to come tomorrow — the action is future and not yet done.
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is important that he come tomorrow.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif présent after impersonal expression
facts.explanation: After impersonal expressions like "il est important que", use subjonctif présent when the desired action is future or ongoing. "qu'il vienne demain" is the correct form.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis surpris(e) que … (choose the correct concordance form)
Context: It is now past 9 a.m. and you are surprised he already arrived.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am astonished that he arrived before 9 a.m.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé after emotion verb
facts.explanation: The arrival is a completed past event. After emotion verbs (être surpris, être content, regretter), use subjonctif passé when the subordinate action happened before the main clause moment.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux qu'elle {___} la vérité.
(I want her to tell the truth.)
Context: The telling is desired but hasn't happened yet.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want her to tell the truth.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif présent after vouloir que
facts.explanation: "dise" (subjonctif présent) follows "vouloir que" when the desired action is future or anticipated. Never use subjonctif passé here — the action hasn't happened yet.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je regrette qu'il {___} sans me dire au revoir.
(I regret that he left without saying goodbye.)
Context: He has already left — the departure is finished.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I regret that he left without saying goodbye.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé after regretter que
facts.explanation: "soit parti" (subjonctif passé) is required: the departure is a completed past action. "regretter que" + subj passé when the regretted event preceded the moment of regret.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut … (choose the correct concordance form)
Context: You are telling someone she must leave right now — the leaving is imminent, not yet done.
facts.sentenceTranslation: She must leave now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif présent (simultané/immédiat)
facts.explanation: When the subordinate action is simultaneous with or immediately following the main clause, use subjonctif présent. "qu'elle parte maintenant" is correct — she hasn't left yet.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis étonné(e) … (choose the correct concordance form)
Context: You arrived and discovered she had already left before you.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am astonished that she left before me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé (antériorité)
facts.explanation: The departure happened before the moment of astonishment — it is an anterior completed action. "qu'elle soit partie avant moi" uses subjonctif passé (être verb: soit + partie with feminine agreement).

facts.quizQuestion:
Sa mère exige … (choose the correct concordance form)
Context: The homework is not done — the mother demands it be done.
facts.sentenceTranslation: His mother demands that he do his homework.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif présent after exiger que
facts.explanation: The demanded action (doing homework) is pending/ongoing. "qu'il fasse ses devoirs" uses subjonctif présent of faire. Exiger que always takes the subjunctive for desired future action.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sa mère est contente … (choose the correct concordance form)
Context: The mother is happy now because he already finished his homework earlier.
facts.sentenceTranslation: His mother is happy that he has done his homework.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé after être content(e) que
facts.explanation: The homework was completed before the mother's happiness — a completed prior action. "qu'il ait fait ses devoirs" uses subjonctif passé of faire (avoir verb: ait + fait).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est dommage qu'elle ne {___} pas à la fête.
(It's a shame she won't come to the party.)
Context: The party is tonight — she hasn't come yet.
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a shame she isn't coming to the party.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif présent after il est dommage que
facts.explanation: "vienne" (subjonctif présent de venir) is correct. The regretted action is future/anticipated. Subjonctif présent follows impersonal expressions when the subordinate action is simultaneous or future.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est dommage qu'elle {___} sans nous prévenir.
(It's a shame she left without warning us.)
Context: She has already left.
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a shame she left without warning us.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé after il est dommage que (antériorité)
facts.explanation: "soit partie" (subjonctif passé de partir) expresses a completed anterior action. The departure happened before the moment of regret. Partir takes être as auxiliary, so agreement applies: partie (f.).

facts.quizQuestion:
Bien qu'il {___} fatigué, il continue à travailler.
(Although he is tired, he keeps working.)
Context: He is tired right now, at this moment.
facts.sentenceTranslation: Although he is tired, he keeps working.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif présent after bien que (simultané)
facts.explanation: "soit" (subjonctif présent d'être) is correct. The tiredness is simultaneous with the working. "Bien que" always triggers the subjunctive; the present form covers simultaneous or ongoing states.

facts.quizQuestion:
Bien qu'il {___} toute la nuit, il ne comprend toujours pas.
(Although he studied all night, he still doesn't understand.)
Context: The studying is finished — it happened last night.
facts.sentenceTranslation: Although he studied all night, he still doesn't understand.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé after bien que (antériorité)
facts.explanation: "ait étudié" (subjonctif passé d'étudier) expresses a completed anterior action. When the bien que clause describes something that finished before the main clause, use subjonctif passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
In modern spoken French, which subjunctive tense replaces the subjonctif imparfait after a past-tense main verb?
(e.g., Il voulait que tu… — what follows in modern usage?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He wanted you to come. → Il voulait que tu viennes. (modern rule)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — modern rule: subj présent replaces subj imparfait
facts.explanation: In modern French (spoken and written), the subjonctif imparfait (e.g., vinsses, fût) has been replaced by subjonctif présent even after a past-tense main verb. Only literary texts still use subj imparfait.

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of être (3rd person singular) found in 19th-century French texts.
Modern equivalent: soit
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that it were / that it might be (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — fût (être, 3sg) — recognition
facts.explanation: "fût" is the subjonctif imparfait of être (3rd person singular). Formed from passé simple stem fus- + -t (with circumflex accent). Modern French replaces this with "soit" (subj présent).
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of avoir (3rd person singular) found in 19th-century French texts.
Modern equivalent: ait
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that he/she might have (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — eût (avoir, 3sg) — recognition
facts.explanation: "eût" is the subjonctif imparfait of avoir (3rd person singular). Stem eu- from passé simple + -t (with circumflex accent). In modern French this is replaced by "ait" (subj présent).
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of être (3rd person plural) found in 19th-century French texts.
Modern equivalent: soient
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that they might be (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — fussent (être, 3pl) — recognition
facts.explanation: "fussent" is the subjonctif imparfait of être (3rd person plural). Stem fuss- + -ent. Modern spoken French uses "soient" (subj présent) in all registers except formal literary writing.
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of avoir (3rd person plural) found in 19th-century French texts.
Modern equivalent: aient
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that they might have (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — eussent (avoir, 3pl) — recognition
facts.explanation: "eussent" is the subjonctif imparfait of avoir (3rd person plural). Stem euss- + -ent. In modern French, "aient" (subj présent) replaces this in all non-literary contexts.
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of faire (3rd person singular) found in classical French prose.
Modern equivalent: fasse
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that he/she might do/make (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — fît (faire, 3sg) — recognition
facts.explanation: "fît" is the subjonctif imparfait of faire (3rd person singular). Passé simple stem fi- + -t (with circumflex accent). Modern French replaces this with "fasse" (subj présent).
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of venir (3rd person singular) found in 19th-century French texts.
Modern equivalent: vienne
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that he/she might come (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — vînt (venir, 3sg) — recognition
facts.explanation: "vînt" is the subjonctif imparfait of venir (3rd person singular). Passé simple stem vin- + -t (with circumflex accent). Modern French uses "vienne" (subj présent) outside literary registers.
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of pouvoir (3rd person singular) found in classical French prose.
Modern equivalent: puisse
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that he/she might be able to (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — pût (pouvoir, 3sg) — recognition
facts.explanation: "pût" is the subjonctif imparfait of pouvoir (3rd person singular). Passé simple stem pu- + -t (with circumflex accent). In modern spoken French, "puisse" (subj présent) is always used instead.
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Identify the literary subjonctif imparfait form of vouloir (3rd person singular) found in 19th-century French texts.
Modern equivalent: veuille
facts.sentenceTranslation: ...that he/she might want / be willing to (literary)
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — voulût (vouloir, 3sg) — recognition
facts.explanation: "voulût" is the subjonctif imparfait of vouloir (3rd person singular). Passé simple stem voul- + -ût. Modern French uses "veuille" (subj présent) in all everyday registers.
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Read this 19th-century sentence and identify the literary subjunctive form:
"Il cherchait un endroit où il {___} seul."
(He was looking for a place where he might be alone.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He was looking for a place where he might be alone.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — fût in literary context (recognition)
facts.explanation: "fût" (subj imparfait d'être, 3sg) is the literary form. The past-tense main verb "cherchait" triggered a past subjunctive under classical concordance rules. Modern French uses "soit" (subj présent) instead.
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Read this literary sentence and identify the subjonctif imparfait form:
"Elle doutait qu'il {___} la force de continuer."
(She doubted that he might have the strength to go on.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She doubted that he might have the strength to go on.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — eût in literary context (recognition)
facts.explanation: "eût" (subj imparfait d'avoir, 3sg) is the literary form triggered by the past-tense "doutait". In modern French, the rule is to use subjonctif présent ("ait") even after past-tense verbs.
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Il craignait que tu {___} en retard.
(He feared you would be late.)
Context: Modern spoken French rule — which subjunctive form follows "il craignait que"?
facts.sentenceTranslation: He feared you would be late.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — modern rule: subj présent after past verb (spoken French)
facts.explanation: In modern spoken French, "sois" (subjonctif présent) is used even after a past-tense trigger like "craignait". The old literary rule required subj imparfait ("fusses"), but this is archaic in everyday use.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je suis content(e) qu'elle {___} le prix.
(I am happy that she won the prize.)
Context: The prize was awarded yesterday — the winning is a completed past fact.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am happy that she won the prize.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Concordance des temps — subjonctif passé after être content(e) que (antériorité)
facts.explanation: "ait gagné" (subjonctif passé de gagner) is required because the winning happened before the moment of happiness. Subjonctif passé = ait + participe passé (avoir verb, no agreement needed here).

facts.quizQuestion: In the Tatoeba sentence "Bien qu'il plût, je devais sortir", what tense is "plût" and in which register is it used?
facts.sentenceTranslation: Although it was raining, I had to go out.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Subjonctif imparfait — plût (pleuvoir) — literary register identification
facts.explanation: "plût" is the subjonctif imparfait of pleuvoir (to rain). The circumflex distinguishes it from "plut" (passé simple indicatif). This form appears only in literary/formal written French; modern speech uses "pleuve" (subj présent).
facts.visualDescription: French grammar literary form recognition card

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans son interruption, {___} pendant une heure.
(Without her interruption, I would have spoken for an hour.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without her interruption, I would have spoken for an hour.
facts.grammarPointLabel: parler (je) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: j'aurais parlé — conditionnel passé: avoir au conditionnel présent (j'aurais) + participe passé (parlé). Expresses a counterfactual past event.

facts.quizQuestion:
Avec plus de temps, {___} ce projet à l'heure.
(With more time, you would have finished this project on time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: With more time, you would have finished this project on time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: finir (tu) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: tu aurais fini — conditionnel passé: avoir au conditionnel présent (tu aurais) + participe passé (fini). Counterfactual past with -ir verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
S'il avait su, {___} le train de six heures.
(If he had known, he would have taken the six o'clock train.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If he had known, he would have taken the six o'clock train.
facts.grammarPointLabel: prendre (il) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: il aurait pris — conditionnel passé of prendre (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (aurait) + participe passé irrégulier (pris).

facts.quizQuestion:
Si nous avions été là, {___} la vérité.
(If we had been there, we would have told the truth.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If we had been there, we would have told the truth.
facts.grammarPointLabel: dire (nous) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: nous aurions dit — conditionnel passé of dire (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (aurions) + participe passé irrégulier (dit).

facts.quizQuestion:
À ma place, {___} la même chose.
(In my place, you would have done the same thing.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: In my place, you would have done the same thing.
facts.grammarPointLabel: faire (vous) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: vous auriez fait — conditionnel passé of faire (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (auriez) + participe passé irrégulier (fait).

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans la grève, {___} la lettre à temps.
(Without the strike, they would have written the letter in time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without the strike, they would have written the letter in time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: écrire (ils) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: ils auraient écrit — conditionnel passé of écrire (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (auraient) + participe passé irrégulier (écrit).

facts.quizQuestion:
Si elle avait été attentive, {___} l'erreur tout de suite.
(If she had been attentive, she would have seen the mistake right away.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she had been attentive, she would have seen the mistake right away.
facts.grammarPointLabel: voir (elle) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: elle aurait vu — conditionnel passé of voir (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (aurait) + participe passé irrégulier (vu).

facts.quizQuestion:
Avec de meilleures conditions, {___} l'examen.
(With better conditions, I would have passed the exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: With better conditions, I would have passed the exam.
facts.grammarPointLabel: réussir (je) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: j'aurais réussi — conditionnel passé of réussir. Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (j'aurais) + participe passé (réussi). Regular -ir verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si on lui avait expliqué, {___} tout de suite.
(If someone had explained it to her, she would have understood right away.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If someone had explained it to her, she would have understood right away.
facts.grammarPointLabel: comprendre (elle) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: elle aurait compris — conditionnel passé of comprendre (irrég., like prendre). Auxiliary avoir (aurait) + participe passé irrégulier (compris).

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est ce que {___}.
(This is what I would have said.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This is what I would have said.
facts.grammarPointLabel: dire (je) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: j'aurais dit — conditionnel passé of dire (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir (j'aurais) + participe passé irrégulier (dit). Classic counterfactual statement.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans ton aide {___}.
(But for your help, I would have failed.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: But for your help, I would have failed.
facts.grammarPointLabel: échouer (je) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: j'aurais échoué — conditionnel passé of échouer. Regular -er verb: auxiliary avoir (j'aurais) + participe passé (échoué).

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'avais eu le temps, {___} au marché ce matin.
(If I had had time, I would have gone to the market this morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had had time, I would have gone to the market this morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aller (je) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: je serais allé(e) — conditionnel passé with être: auxiliary être au conditionnel (je serais) + participe passé (allé/allée). Aller takes être as auxiliary.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans ce problème, {___} hier.
(Without this problem, you would have left yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without this problem, you would have left yesterday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: partir (tu) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: tu serais parti(e) — conditionnel passé with être: auxiliary être au conditionnel (tu serais) + participe passé (parti/partie). Agreement with subject gender.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu l'avais invitée, {___} à la fête.
(If you had invited her, she would have come to the party.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had invited her, she would have come to the party.
facts.grammarPointLabel: venir (elle) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: elle serait venue — conditionnel passé of venir with être. Feminine subject: participe passé agrees → venue (not venu). Venir always takes être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si le concert avait fini à l'heure, {___} avant minuit.
(If the concert had ended on time, we would have returned before midnight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If the concert had ended on time, we would have returned before midnight.
facts.grammarPointLabel: rentrer (nous) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: nous serions rentrés — conditionnel passé of rentrer with être. Plural masculine/mixed: participe passé takes plural form (rentrés).

facts.quizQuestion:
S'ils n'avaient pas eu d'embouteillage, {___} à l'heure.
(If they had not been stuck in traffic, they would have arrived on time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If they had not been stuck in traffic, they would have arrived on time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: arriver (ils) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: ils seraient arrivés — conditionnel passé of arriver with être. Masculine plural subject: participe passé agrees → arrivés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans ce problème au travail, {___} en France cet été.
(Without this problem at work, she would have returned to France this summer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without this problem at work, she would have returned to France this summer.
facts.grammarPointLabel: retourner (elle) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: elle serait retournée — conditionnel passé of retourner with être. Feminine singular: participe passé agrees → retournée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Par ce beau temps, {___} plus tôt.
(In this nice weather, you would have gone out earlier.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: In this nice weather, you would have gone out earlier.
facts.grammarPointLabel: sortir (vous) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: vous seriez sortis — conditionnel passé of sortir with être. Plural or formal vous: participe passé takes plural form (sortis/sorties).

facts.quizQuestion:
Si ses parents avaient déménagé, {___} à Paris.
(If his parents had moved, he would have been born in Paris.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If his parents had moved, he would have been born in Paris.
facts.grammarPointLabel: naître (il) — Conditionnel passé être
facts.explanation: il serait né — conditionnel passé of naître with être. Naître always takes être. Masculine singular: participe passé né (no agreement marker).

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans l'alarme, {___} trop tard.
(Without the alarm, I would have gotten up too late.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without the alarm, I would have gotten up too late.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se lever (je) — Conditionnel passé pronominal
facts.explanation: je me serais levé(e) — conditionnel passé of pronominal se lever. Pronominal verbs always use être: je me serais + participe passé. Gender agreement applies.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans la pluie, {___} différemment pour la cérémonie.
(Without the rain, she would have dressed differently for the ceremony.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without the rain, she would have dressed differently for the ceremony.
facts.grammarPointLabel: s'habiller (elle) — Conditionnel passé pronominal
facts.explanation: elle se serait habillée — conditionnel passé of pronominal s'habiller with être. Feminine subject: participe passé agrees → habillée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans ce film, {___} plus tôt.
(Without that film, we would have gone to bed earlier.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without that film, we would have gone to bed earlier.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se coucher (nous) — Conditionnel passé pronominal
facts.explanation: nous nous serions couchés — conditionnel passé of pronominal se coucher with être. Plural: participe passé couchés (masculine/mixed) or couchées (all-feminine).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} travailler plus s'il voulait réussir.
(He should have worked harder if he wanted to succeed.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He should have worked harder if he wanted to succeed.
facts.grammarPointLabel: devoir (il) — Conditionnel passé modal: obligation non remplie
facts.explanation: il aurait dû — conditionnel passé of devoir. Auxiliary avoir (aurait) + participe passé (dû). Expresses 'he should have' — a past moral obligation that was not met.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} te présenter avant de commencer.
(You should have introduced yourself before starting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You should have introduced yourself before starting.
facts.grammarPointLabel: devoir (tu) — Conditionnel passé modal: regret
facts.explanation: tu aurais dû — conditionnel passé of devoir. Expresses 'you should have' — a past obligation not fulfilled.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} me déconnecter avant de sauvegarder.
(I shouldn't have logged off before saving.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I shouldn't have logged off before saving.
facts.grammarPointLabel: devoir (je négatif) — Conditionnel passé modal négatif
facts.explanation: je n'aurais pas dû — negated conditionnel passé of devoir. Negation wraps the auxiliary: ne...pas around aurais. Expresses regret for an action taken.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} voir l'exposition — elle était magnifique !
(You ought to have seen the exhibition — it was magnificent!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You ought to have seen the exhibition — it was magnificent!
facts.grammarPointLabel: devoir (tu) — Conditionnel passé modal: conseil rétrospectif
facts.explanation: tu aurais dû — conditionnel passé of devoir. Also rendered as 'you ought to have' — expressing a missed opportunity the speaker regrets on the listener's behalf.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} le faire toi-même — ce n'était pas compliqué.
(You could have done it yourself — it wasn't complicated.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You could have done it yourself — it wasn't complicated.
facts.grammarPointLabel: pouvoir (tu) — Conditionnel passé modal: capacité non utilisée
facts.explanation: tu aurais pu — conditionnel passé of pouvoir. Expresses 'you could have' — an unrealized past ability or possibility.

facts.quizQuestion:
Avec une meilleure organisation, {___} éviter ces problèmes.
(With better organisation, we could have avoided these problems.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: With better organisation, we could have avoided these problems.
facts.grammarPointLabel: pouvoir (nous) — Conditionnel passé modal
facts.explanation: nous aurions pu — conditionnel passé of pouvoir. Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (aurions) + participe passé irrégulier (pu). 'We could have'.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} être là pour te soutenir.
(I would have wanted to be there to support you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would have wanted to be there to support you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: vouloir (je) — Conditionnel passé modal: souhait non réalisé
facts.explanation: j'aurais voulu — conditionnel passé of vouloir. Expresses 'I would have wanted' — a past desire that could not be fulfilled.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} venir à la fête mais ils étaient malades.
(They would have wanted to come to the party but they were ill.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They would have wanted to come to the party but they were ill.
facts.grammarPointLabel: vouloir (ils) — Conditionnel passé modal
facts.explanation: ils auraient voulu — conditionnel passé of vouloir. Auxiliary avoir (auraient) + participe passé (voulu). Unrealized past wish.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} être un oiseau.
(I wish I were a bird. / I would have liked to be a bird.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wish I were a bird.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aimer (je) — Conditionnel passé modal: souhait impossible
facts.explanation: j'aurais aimé — conditionnel passé of aimer. Expresses an impossible or unfulfilled wish about the past. Regular -er verb with auxiliary avoir.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} voir le film avec vous mais je travaillais.
(I would have liked to see the film with you but I was working.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would have liked to see the film with you but I was working.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aimer (je) — Conditionnel passé modal: regret
facts.explanation: j'aurais aimé — conditionnel passé of aimer used to express a missed wish. Common polite or regretful expression in spoken and written French.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} avoir une meilleure mémoire.
(I wish I had a better memory.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wish I had a better memory.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aimer (je) — Conditionnel passé modal: souhait contrefactuel
facts.explanation: j'aurais aimé — conditionnel passé of aimer. Used for a counterfactual wish about a personal trait, equivalent to 'I wish I had…'.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} être plus grand pour jouer au basket.
(I wish I were taller to play basketball.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wish I were taller to play basketball.
facts.grammarPointLabel: aimer (je) — Conditionnel passé: souhait physique impossible
facts.explanation: j'aurais aimé — conditionnel passé of aimer expressing a wish about an unchangeable physical trait. Common register in French for unfulfillable desires.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} rester à la maison plutôt que d'aller à cette réunion.
(She would have preferred to stay home rather than go to that meeting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She would have preferred to stay home rather than go to that meeting.
facts.grammarPointLabel: préférer (elle) — Conditionnel passé modal: préférence non réalisée
facts.explanation: elle aurait préféré — conditionnel passé of préférer. Regular pattern: auxiliary avoir (aurait) + participe passé (préféré). Expresses a past preference that didn't happen.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} me le dire plus tôt — j'aurais pu aider.
(You should have told me sooner — I could have helped.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You should have told me sooner — I could have helped.
facts.grammarPointLabel: devoir (vous) — Conditionnel passé modal
facts.explanation: vous auriez dû — conditionnel passé of devoir. Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (auriez) + participe passé (dû). 'You should have' to a formal or plural addressee.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} partir plus tôt pour éviter les bouchons.
(It would have been necessary to leave earlier to avoid the traffic jams.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It would have been necessary to leave earlier to avoid the traffic jams.
facts.grammarPointLabel: falloir (impersonnel) — Conditionnel passé modal: nécessité
facts.explanation: il aurait fallu — conditionnel passé of impersonal falloir. Always il (impersonal subject). Expresses 'it would have been necessary to' — a past necessity not fulfilled.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} investir davantage dans l'éducation.
(It would have been necessary to invest more in education.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It would have been necessary to invest more in education.
facts.grammarPointLabel: falloir (impersonnel) — Conditionnel passé: regret social/politique
facts.explanation: il aurait fallu — conditionnel passé impersonnel. Used in formal or political discourse to express a past necessity that was not met, often with a critical or regretful tone.

facts.quizQuestion:
Selon des sources proches du dossier, le président {___} vouloir démissionner.
(According to sources close to the matter, the president allegedly declared that he wants to resign.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: According to sources close to the matter, the president allegedly declared that he wants to resign.
facts.grammarPointLabel: déclarer — Conditionnel passé: style journalistique (information non confirmée)
facts.explanation: aurait déclaré — conditionnel passé used in journalism to report unconfirmed information. Known as le conditionnel journalistique — signals the speaker does not vouch for the facts.

facts.quizQuestion:
D'après nos informations, le ministre {___} un accord secret hier soir.
(According to our information, the minister allegedly signed a secret agreement last night.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: According to our information, the minister allegedly signed a secret agreement last night.
facts.grammarPointLabel: signer — Conditionnel passé: style journalistique (allégation)
facts.explanation: aurait signé — conditionnel passé journalistique. Used with verbs of action to report unverified news without committing to its truth. Equivalent to 'allegedly' in English press.

facts.quizQuestion:
Selon les syndicats, la direction {___} plus de deux cents employés sans préavis.
(According to the unions, management allegedly dismissed over two hundred employees without notice.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: According to the unions, management allegedly dismissed over two hundred employees without notice.
facts.grammarPointLabel: licencier — Conditionnel passé: style journalistique
facts.explanation: aurait licencié — conditionnel passé journalistique. The speaker reports an allegation (syndicats as source) without confirming it. This distancing use is a key B2 register feature.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'avais connu ton numéro, {___} t'appeler directement.
(If I had known your number, I could have called you directly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had known your number, I could have called you directly.
facts.grammarPointLabel: pouvoir (je) — Conditionnel passé modal: possibilité non réalisée
facts.explanation: j'aurais pu — conditionnel passé of pouvoir in type-3 conditional: si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé. 'I could have'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu m'avais envoyé un message, {___} ta réponse à temps.
(If you had sent me a message, I would have received your reply in time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had sent me a message, I would have received your reply in time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: recevoir (je) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: j'aurais reçu — conditionnel passé of recevoir (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir (j'aurais) + participe passé irrégulier (reçu). Part of a type-3 conditional structure.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans la pandémie, {___} le projet en œuvre dès janvier.
(Without the pandemic, they would have implemented the project from January.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without the pandemic, they would have implemented the project from January.
facts.grammarPointLabel: mettre (ils) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: ils auraient mis — conditionnel passé of mettre (irrég.). Auxiliary avoir (auraient) + participe passé irrégulier (mis). Expresses a counterfactual past action.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu lui avais rappelé, {___} du rendez-vous.
(If you had reminded him, he would have remembered the appointment.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had reminded him, he would have remembered the appointment.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se souvenir (il) — Conditionnel passé pronominal
facts.explanation: il se serait souvenu — conditionnel passé of pronominal se souvenir (always pronominal). Uses être as auxiliary. Masculine subject: no agreement marking on souvenu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Sans les restrictions, {___} le restaurant dès le printemps.
(Without the restrictions, we would have opened the restaurant from spring.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Without the restrictions, we would have opened the restaurant from spring.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ouvrir (nous) — Conditionnel passé avoir
facts.explanation: nous aurions ouvert — conditionnel passé of ouvrir (irrég.). Participe passé is ouvert (not *ouvri). Auxiliary avoir au conditionnel (aurions).

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu avais étudié, tu {___} l'examen.
(If you had studied, you would have passed the exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had studied, you would have passed the exam.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurais réussi — conditionnel passé of réussir. The consequence clause uses avoir in conditionnel présent (aurais) + past participle réussi. The si-clause uses plus-que-parfait.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'avais su, je {___}.
(If I had known, I would have come.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had known, I would have come.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — venir (être verb) in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: serais venu — conditionnel passé of venir (être verb). Je serais venu(e): être in conditionnel présent (serais) + past participle venu, agreeing with subject gender.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si elle était partie plus tôt, elle {___} le train.
(If she had left earlier, she would have caught the train.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she had left earlier, she would have caught the train.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — prendre in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait pris — conditionnel passé of prendre (irregular past participle pris). Elle aurait pris: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + pris.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je serais venu si j'{___}.
(I would have come if I had known.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would have come if I had known.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — consequence clause first, si-clause second
facts.explanation: avais su — plus-que-parfait of savoir. J'avais su: avoir in imparfait (avais) + past participle su. Either clause may come first; when the si-clause follows, no comma is used.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si nous avions fait attention, nous {___} cette erreur.
(If we had paid attention, we would have avoided this mistake.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If we had paid attention, we would have avoided this mistake.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — éviter in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurions évité — conditionnel passé of éviter. Nous aurions évité: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurions) + past participle évité.

facts.quizQuestion:
S'il avait travaillé plus dur, il {___} le match.
(If he had worked harder, he would have won the match.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If he had worked harder, he would have won the match.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — gagner in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait gagné — conditionnel passé of gagner. Il aurait gagné: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + past participle gagné.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si vous aviez appelé, ils {___} vous aider.
(If you had called, they would have been able to help you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had called, they would have been able to help you.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — pouvoir in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: auraient pu — conditionnel passé of pouvoir (irregular past participle pu). Ils auraient pu + infinitive: unrealized possibility in the past.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si le bus était parti à l'heure, ils {___} à temps.
(If the bus had left on time, they would have arrived on time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If the bus had left on time, they would have arrived on time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — arriver (être verb) in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: seraient arrivés — conditionnel passé of arriver (être verb). Ils seraient arrivés: être in conditionnel présent (seraient) + past participle arrivés, agreeing with plural ils.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} la vérité si tu le lui avais demandé.
(She would have told the truth if you had asked her.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She would have told the truth if you had asked her.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — dire in conditionnel passé, consequence first
facts.explanation: aurait dit — conditionnel passé of dire (irregular past participle dit). Elle aurait dit: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + dit. Consequence clause comes first here.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si nous avions su le prix, nous {___} autant.
(If we had known the price, we would not have spent so much.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If we had known the price, we would not have spent so much.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — negated conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: n'aurions pas dépensé — negated conditionnel passé of dépenser. Negation wraps the auxiliary: nous n'aurions pas + past participle dépensé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'avais eu le temps, j'{___} cette exposition.
(If I had had the time, I would have seen this exhibition.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had had the time, I would have seen this exhibition.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — voir in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurais vu — conditionnel passé of voir (irregular past participle vu). J'aurais vu: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurais) + vu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si on avait prévu ce problème, il {___} agir plus vite.
(If this problem had been foreseen, it would have been necessary to act faster.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If this problem had been foreseen, it would have been necessary to act faster.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — falloir (impersonal) in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait fallu — conditionnel passé of falloir (impersonal). Il aurait fallu + infinitive. Falloir has irregular past participle fallu; only the il form exists.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il aurait réussi s'il {___} davantage.
(He would have succeeded if he had studied more.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He would have succeeded if he had studied more.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — identifying the plus-que-parfait in si-clause
facts.explanation: avait étudié — plus-que-parfait of étudier. S'il avait étudié: avoir in imparfait (avait) + past participle étudié. The consequence clause comes first here.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si on lui avait donné le choix, elle {___} autrement.
(If she had been given the choice, she would have chosen differently.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she had been given the choice, she would have chosen differently.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — choisir in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait choisi — conditionnel passé of choisir. Elle aurait choisi: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + past participle choisi.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu avais accepté leur offre, ta vie {___} différente.
(If you had accepted their offer, your life would have been different.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had accepted their offer, your life would have been different.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — être in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait été — conditionnel passé of être (irregular past participle été). Ta vie aurait été: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + été.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'avais étudié la médecine, je {___} maintenant.
(If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past si-clause → present consequence
facts.explanation: serais médecin — conditionnel présent of être. The si-clause uses plus-que-parfait (past hypothesis), but the consequence uses conditionnel présent because the result is a present state (being a doctor now). This is the defining feature of the mixed conditional.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu n'avais pas raté ton avion, tu {___}.
(If you hadn't missed your plane, you would already be in Paris.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you hadn't missed your plane, you would already be in Paris.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past si-clause → present location consequence
facts.explanation: serais déjà à Paris — conditionnel présent of être + location. The past event (missing the flight) produces a present consequence (not being in Paris yet). Conditionnel présent, not conditionnel passé, signals the present-tense result.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu avais suivi les cours assidûment, tu {___}.
(If you had attended classes regularly, you would speak better French now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had attended classes regularly, you would speak better French now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past learning → present ability consequence
facts.explanation: parlerais mieux français — conditionnel présent of parler. The past study (hypothetical) produces a present skill level (speaking better now). Conditionnel présent marks this ongoing consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
S'il avait pris soin de sa santé, il {___} aujourd'hui.
(If he had taken care of his health, he would feel better today.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If he had taken care of his health, he would feel better today.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past habits → present health state
facts.explanation: se sentirait mieux — conditionnel présent of se sentir (reflexive). Past health habits (hypothetical) → current physical wellbeing. Conditionnel présent, not passé, marks the present-state consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si nous avions économisé, nous {___} maintenant.
(If we had saved money, we would be able to travel now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If we had saved money, we would be able to travel now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past saving → present ability with pouvoir
facts.explanation: pourrions voyager — conditionnel présent of pouvoir + infinitive. Past saving (hypothetical) → present ability to travel. Nous pourrions: conditionnel présent marks the current capability.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si elle avait accepté le poste, elle {___} en ce moment.
(If she had accepted the position, she would be working in Lyon right now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she had accepted the position, she would be working in Lyon right now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past decision → present ongoing situation
facts.explanation: travaillerait à Lyon — conditionnel présent of travailler + location. A past decision (accepting the post) produces a present ongoing consequence (working there now). Conditionnel présent signals the present-tense result.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'avais mieux dormi hier soir, je {___} maintenant.
(If I had slept better last night, I would be less tired now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had slept better last night, I would be less tired now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past night's sleep → present energy level
facts.explanation: serais moins fatigué — conditionnel présent of être + adjective. Yesterday's sleep quality (past hypothesis) → today's energy level (present state). Conditionnel présent, not passé, marks the ongoing consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si elle n'avait pas quitté Paris, elle {___}.
(If she hadn't left Paris, she would still be living there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she hadn't left Paris, she would still be living there.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past departure → present ongoing residence
facts.explanation: habiterait encore là-bas — conditionnel présent of habiter. The past departure (hypothetically not occurring) → present ongoing residence. Conditionnel présent marks this current-state consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu avais voulu attraper ce bus, tu {___} plus tôt.
(If you had wanted to catch that bus, you would have had to leave earlier.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had wanted to catch that bus, you would have had to leave earlier.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — devoir in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait dû partir — conditionnel passé of devoir + infinitive. Tu aurais dû partir: devoir in conditionnel passé (aurait dû) + infinitive partir. Expresses unrealized obligation in the past.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'avais eu plus de temps libre, j'{___} voyager davantage.
(If I had had more free time, I would have liked to travel more.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had had more free time, I would have liked to travel more.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — aimer in conditionnel passé (wish/preference)
facts.explanation: aurais aimé — conditionnel passé of aimer. J'aurais aimé + infinitive expresses an unrealized wish or preference in the past. Pattern sourced from Tatoeba example 12077.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si elle avait grandi bilingue, elle {___} travailler à l'international maintenant.
(If she had grown up bilingual, she would want to work internationally now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she had grown up bilingual, she would want to work internationally now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — past upbringing → present desire consequence
facts.explanation: voudrait — conditionnel présent of vouloir. A past formative experience (growing up bilingual) → present desire or inclination. Conditionnel présent marks this current-state want.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu lui avais expliqué la situation, il {___} mieux.
(If you had explained the situation to him, he would have understood better.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you had explained the situation to him, he would have understood better.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — comprendre in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait compris — conditionnel passé of comprendre (irregular past participle compris). Il aurait compris: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + compris.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si elle avait appris à cuisiner enfant, elle {___} mieux maintenant.
(If she had learned to cook as a child, she would know how to cook better now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If she had learned to cook as a child, she would know how to cook better now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Mixed conditional — childhood past → present skill with savoir
facts.explanation: saurait cuisiner — conditionnel présent of savoir + infinitive. Past learning (childhood) → present ability or knowledge. Savoir in conditionnel présent marks the current skill state.

facts.quizQuestion:
S'il avait mangé sainement toute sa vie, il {___} plus longtemps.
(If he had eaten healthily all his life, he would have lived longer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If he had eaten healthily all his life, he would have lived longer.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — vivre in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: aurait vécu — conditionnel passé of vivre (irregular past participle vécu). Il aurait vécu: avoir in conditionnel présent (aurait) + vécu.

facts.quizQuestion:
S'ils avaient écouté les conseils, ils {___} cette catastrophe.
(If they had listened to the advice, they would have been able to avoid this disaster.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If they had listened to the advice, they would have been able to avoid this disaster.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si-clause Type 3 — pouvoir + infinitive in conditionnel passé
facts.explanation: auraient pu éviter — conditionnel passé of pouvoir + infinitive. Ils auraient pu éviter: avoir in conditionnel présent (auraient) + pu (irregular) + infinitive éviter. Modal in conditionnel passé expresses unrealized possibility.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je travaille à Paris. » → Rapporté: Il a dit qu'il {___} à Paris.
(He said that he worked in Paris.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He said that he worked in Paris.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent → imparfait
facts.explanation: travaillait — backshift: présent (travaille) → imparfait (travaillait) quand le verbe introducteur est au passé (a dit).

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je suis fatigué. » → Rapporté: Il a dit qu'il {___}.
(He said that he was tired.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He said that he was tired.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent être → imparfait
facts.explanation: était fatigué — être au présent devient être à l'imparfait dans le discours rapporté au passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je comprends la situation. » → Rapporté: Il a affirmé qu'il {___} la situation.
(He stated that he understood the situation.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He stated that he understood the situation.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent → imparfait (comprendre)
facts.explanation: comprenait — comprendre au présent (comprend) → imparfait (comprenait) au discours rapporté passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je préfère rester. » → Rapporté: Elle a déclaré qu'elle {___} rester.
(She declared that she preferred to stay.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She declared that she preferred to stay.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent → imparfait (préférer)
facts.explanation: préférait — préférer au présent (préfère) → imparfait (préférait) quand le verbe introducteur est au passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je ne sais pas. » → Rapporté: Il a répondu qu'il {___}.
(He replied that he didn't know.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He replied that he didn't know.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent négatif → imparfait négatif
facts.explanation: ne savait pas — savoir au présent (ne sait pas) → imparfait négatif (ne savait pas) en discours rapporté.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « J'habite là depuis dix ans. » → Rapporté: Elle a précisé qu'elle {___} là depuis dix ans.
(She specified that she had been living there for ten years.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She specified that she had been living there for ten years.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent duratif → imparfait
facts.explanation: habitait — habiter au présent (habite) → imparfait (habitait). L'imparfait maintient le sens duratif.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je ne veux pas y aller. » → Rapporté: Il a avoué qu'il ne {___} pas y aller.
(He admitted that he didn't want to go there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He admitted that he didn't want to go there.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent vouloir → imparfait
facts.explanation: voulait — vouloir au présent (veut) → imparfait (voulait) en discours rapporté passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je pense que c'est une bonne idée. » → Rapporté: Elle a dit qu'elle {___} que c'était une bonne idée.
(She said she thought it was a good idea.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She said she thought it was a good idea.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent penser → imparfait
facts.explanation: pensait — penser au présent (pense) → imparfait (pensait). Double backshift: pense → pensait, est → était.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je peux t'aider. » → Rapporté: Il a dit qu'il {___} m'aider.
(He said that he could help me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He said that he could help me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent pouvoir → imparfait
facts.explanation: pouvait — pouvoir au présent (peut) → imparfait (pouvait) en discours rapporté passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je cherche un appartement. » → Rapporté: Elle a expliqué qu'elle {___} un appartement.
(She explained that she was looking for an apartment.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She explained that she was looking for an apartment.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: présent → imparfait (chercher)
facts.explanation: cherchait — chercher au présent (cherche) → imparfait (cherchait) dans le discours rapporté passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Viens ! » → Rapporté: Il m'a dit {___}.
(He told me to come.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He told me to come.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: impératif → de + infinitif
facts.explanation: de venir — l'impératif au discours rapporté passé devient de + infinitif. Structure: dire/demander + de + infinitif.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Taisez-vous ! » → Rapporté: Le professeur leur a dit {___}.
(The teacher told them to be quiet.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The teacher told them to be quiet.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: impératif pronominal → de + infinitif pronominal
facts.explanation: de se taire — impératif (taisez-vous) → de + infinitif pronominal (de se taire). Le pronom réfléchi devient se.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Ne pars pas ! » → Rapporté: Elle lui a demandé {___}.
(She asked him not to leave.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She asked him not to leave.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: impératif négatif → de ne pas + infinitif
facts.explanation: de ne pas partir — impératif négatif → de ne pas + infinitif. La négation encadre l'infinitif: ne pas + infinitif.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « J'ai fini le rapport. » → Rapporté: Il a dit qu'il {___} le rapport.
(He said that he had finished the report.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He said that he had finished the report.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: passé composé → plus-que-parfait
facts.explanation: avait fini — passé composé (a fini) → plus-que-parfait (avait fini) en discours rapporté passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je suis arrivée en retard. » → Rapporté: Elle a avoué qu'elle {___}.
(She admitted that she had arrived late.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She admitted that she had arrived late.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: passé composé (être) → plus-que-parfait
facts.explanation: était arrivée en retard — PC avec être (est arrivée) → PQP avec être (était arrivée). Accord du PP avec le sujet.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « J'ai oublié mon passeport. » → Rapporté: Il a expliqué qu'il {___} son passeport.
(He explained that he had forgotten his passport.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He explained that he had forgotten his passport.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: passé composé → plus-que-parfait (oublier)
facts.explanation: avait oublié — oublier au PC (a oublié) → PQP (avait oublié). Auxiliaire avoir, PP oublié invariable.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « J'ai déjà vu ce film. » → Rapporté: Elle a affirmé qu'elle {___} ce film.
(She stated that she had already seen that film.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She stated that she had already seen that film.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: passé composé → plus-que-parfait (voir)
facts.explanation: avait déjà vu — voir au PC (a déjà vu) → PQP (avait déjà vu). L'adverbe déjà se maintient.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je me suis trompé. » → Rapporté: Il a reconnu qu'il {___}.
(He acknowledged that he had been wrong.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He acknowledged that he had been wrong.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: PC pronominal → PQP pronominal
facts.explanation: s'était trompé — verbe pronominal: PC (s'est trompé) → PQP (s'était trompé). Auxiliaire être au PQP.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je n'ai pas compris. » → Rapporté: Elle a admis qu'elle {___}.
(She admitted that she hadn't understood.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She admitted that she hadn't understood.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: PC négatif → PQP négatif
facts.explanation: n'avait pas compris — PC négatif (n'a pas compris) → PQP négatif (n'avait pas compris). La négation se place entre auxiliaire et PP.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « Je suis parti à midi. » → Rapporté: Il a précisé qu'il {___} à midi.
(He specified that he had left at noon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He specified that he had left at noon.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: PC (partir, être) → PQP
facts.explanation: était parti — partir au PC (est parti) → PQP (était parti). Verbe de mouvement avec être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Direct: « J'ai lu ton message. » → Rapporté: Elle a dit qu'elle {___} mon message.
(She said that she had read my message.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She said that she had read my message.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté au passé: passé composé → plus-que-parfait (lire)
facts.explanation: avait lu — lire au PC (a lu) → PQP (avait lu). PP irrégulier: lu.

facts.quizQuestion:
